findings.description: The map image in the HTML contains visible text (title 'SURFACE WATER ASSESSMENT', site info, notes, references, scale/date/project info) and the same content is repeated as HTML text below the image, creating reading-order duplication.
findings.evidence: PDF: Single integrated figure page with map, legend, notes, references, and title block. HTML: Map rendered as an image that still contains all that text, followed by a full HTML-text rendition of the same title, notes, references, scale, date, project number, and figure number below the image.

findings.description: The primary analytical data of the figure — the five surface water sample result callouts (SW-1/DUP-6102025-SD, SW-2, SW-3, SW-4, SW-5) with contaminant concentrations and risk assessments — are present as text in the PDF but entirely absent from the HTML body. Only the embedded map image (which has been stripped) contains this data.
findings.evidence: PDF contains text blocks such as 'SW-1/DUP-6102025-SD ug/L / Copper 4.69 / Lead 0.868 J / Risk Assessment CR: 2.4E-08 HI: 0.39', 'SW-3 ug/L / Tetrachloroethene 12 / Copper 5.86 / Risk Assessment CR: 1.6E-07 HI: 0.091', etc. None of these five data blocks appear anywhere in the HTML text.

findings.description: Five sample-result data tables (one per surface water location) present in the PDF are completely missing from the HTML. The HTML contains zero tables.
findings.evidence: PDF has tabular data for SW-1 (2 analytes + risk), SW-2 (2 analytes + risk), SW-3 (2 analytes + risk), SW-4 (2 analytes + risk), SW-5 (2 analytes + risk). HTML has no <table> elements at all.

findings.description: The NCDEQ ID number is missing one zero. The PDF has five zeros ('NONCD00000870') while the HTML has four zeros ('NONCD0000870').
findings.evidence: PDF: 'NCDEQ ID NO. NONCD00000870'; HTML: 'NCDEQ ID NO. NONCD0000870'
